pr_title: feat(upgrade): PHP 8.4 + Symfony 6.4 baseline, Goutte excision, Rector modernization (PR2/3)

exit_condition.reason: 5 P1 (0 critical, 5 high) findings remain open. Note: 3 of 5 are pre-existing (exec, XXE, UriRetriever SSRF) explicitly deferred to PR3 per the plan; they surfaced in PR2 only because Rector touched the files.

findings.title: DOMNodeList case-inconsistent import + docblock in XmlContext
findings.author: pattern-recognition-specialist
findings.description: src/Context/XmlContext.php:8 has `use DomNodeList;` (camelCase) and line 42 has `@return DomNodeList`. Same class of bug as the Dom.php fix that was manually applied during Rector. PHP allows the mismatch case-insensitively, but the import inconsistency is a maintenance smell and Rector's import pass was confused by it in Dom.php.
findings.recommendation: Change both to `DOMNodeList` (uppercase) to match PHP's canonical naming and the rest of the file. Fix in PR2.

findings.title: declare(strict_types=1) applied unevenly across Rector-touched files
findings.author: pattern-recognition-specialist + security-sentinel
findings.description: Pattern reviewer found strict_types in only 6/29 Rector-touched files (all in src/HttpCall/ root). Missing from src/Context/*, src/Json/*, src/Xml/*, src/HttpCall/Request/BrowserKit.php, and all tests/units/ files. Rector's type-declarations set or code-quality set was expected to add it uniformly.
findings.recommendation: Investigate why Rector skipped these files. Either re-run Rector with a set that adds strict_types uniformly (DeclareStrictTypesRector is in CodeQuality set) or add the declaration manually to the 23 files missing it. Fix in PR2.

findings.title: exec() receives unescaped user input (pre-existing, PR3-deferred)
findings.author: security-sentinel
findings.description: `exec($cmd, ...)` runs Gherkin :command verbatim with no escapeshellarg/escapeshellcmd. Pre-existing issue; surfaced as in_scope because Rector modified this file in PR2. Already on PR3's security-audit checklist. Not a PR2 regression.
findings.recommendation: Deferred to PR3 (security-audit phase). The plan specifies a path-containment helper + `escapeshellarg` wrapping for the `exec()` call site as part of that audit. Do not block PR2 merge on this.

findings.title: LIBXML_PARSEHUGE XXE surface (pre-existing, PR3-deferred)
findings.author: security-sentinel
findings.description: `$dom->loadXML($content, LIBXML_PARSEHUGE)` with no LIBXML_NONET, no external-entity-loader shim. LIBXML_PARSEHUGE actually WIDENS the DoS/XXE attack surface. `validateXsd`/`validateNg` also pull external schemas. Pre-existing; surfaced in_scope because Rector touched this file.
findings.recommendation: Deferred to PR3. The plan specifies: install `libxml_set_external_entity_loader(static fn() => null)` in a @BeforeSuite hook, drop LIBXML_PARSEHUGE, add LIBXML_NONET + LIBXML_NO_XXE (on PHP 8.4 with libxml 2.13+). Do not block PR2 merge.

findings.title: SSRF via UriRetriever in JsonInspector (pre-existing, PR3-deferred)
findings.author: security-sentinel
findings.description: `new SchemaStorage(new UriRetriever, new UriResolver)` resolves $ref from any scheme by default — http, https, file. Malicious feature JSON can $ref http://169.254.169.254/ or file:///etc/passwd. Pre-existing; in_scope because Rector touched this file.
findings.recommendation: Deferred to PR3. The plan specifies a `SafeUriRetriever` with a schema-root allowlist, injected via a `protected function createSchemaStorage()` override hook on JsonInspector (architecture review #1 from deepen-plan). Do not block PR2 merge.

findings.title: readonly Request::$mink may break subclasses that reassign in ctor
findings.author: architecture-strategist
findings.description: Rector-applied `private readonly Mink $mink` on Request.php. Any downstream subclass that overrode the constructor and reassigned $this->mink (legal in 4.x) now throws a fatal at class-load. For dev-master consumers, this is a silent BC break.
findings.recommendation: Either: (a) remove `readonly` from Request::$mink (accept the modest encapsulation loss), or (b) document explicitly in UPGRADE-5.0.md and leave it (readonly is safer in practice; most subclasses don't need to reassign). Recommend (b) with doc update.

findings.title: Missing `composer audit` step in CI
findings.author: security-sentinel + performance-oracle
findings.description: CI runs composer validate + install + atoum but no `composer audit`. Scrutinizer was deleted in this PR; CodeQL comes in PR3. The gap leaves PR2 with zero automated vulnerability scanning. Adding `composer audit --no-dev` after install is one line.
findings.recommendation: Add `- name: composer audit\n  run: composer audit --no-dev --no-interaction` step to ci.yml. Fix in PR2.

findings.title: composer install without lock = non-reproducible CI
findings.author: performance-oracle + architecture-strategist + security-sentinel
findings.description: composer.lock is .gitignored. CI runs `composer install` which, without a lock, falls back to update behavior (Composer 2.x warns but proceeds). Every CI run resolves deps fresh; 'green on CI' is a moving target, and a malicious minor in any direct dep lands silently. The compound doc explicitly blessed 'no lock for libraries' but CI reproducibility is a separate concern.
findings.recommendation: Two clean paths: (a) commit composer.lock (standard in 2026 for libraries with CI; update the compound doc with the nuance that consumers still resolve their own tree), or (b) switch to `composer update --prefer-dist --no-interaction --no-progress` explicitly and accept non-determinism. Recommend (a). Fix in PR2.

findings.title: CRLF injection possible via setHttpHeader (pre-existing, PR3-deferred)
findings.author: security-sentinel
findings.description: setHttpHeader($name, $value) passes $value unvalidated to setServerParameter. A Gherkin step with $value containing `\r\nInjected-Header: evil` splits the header. Symfony BrowserKit normalizes $_SERVER keys but not values.
findings.recommendation: Deferred to PR3. The plan specifies a `Behatch\HttpCall\Request\HeaderValidator` (next to its consumer per architecture review) with RFC 7230 token grammar on the name and field-value validation on the value, explicit reject on CR/LF/NUL/obs-fold. Do not block PR2 merge.

findings.title: Dead PHP 5 version check in Extension::initialize
findings.author: architecture-strategist
findings.description: `if (PHP_MAJOR_VERSION === 5)` deprecation emitter in Extension::initialize is dead code given `php: ^8.4` floor. Rector's deadCode set should have removed it but apparently didn't detect the runtime-constant comparison.
findings.recommendation: Remove the PHP 5 check and its trigger_error payload. Fix in PR2 (one-commit cleanup).

findings.title: CI matrix PHP 8.4 only; no PHP 8.5 forward-compat signal
findings.author: architecture-strategist
findings.description: Matrix is php: ['8.4'] only. PHP 8.5 is out and available in shivammathur/setup-php. Adding '8.5' to the matrix is one line and gives forward-compat signal essentially free.
findings.recommendation: Change matrix to `php: ['8.4', '8.5']` with `fail-fast: false` (already set). Fix in PR2.

findings.title: Hard-fail on session: symfony2 vs soft deprecation alias
findings.author: architecture-strategist (reversing simplicity-reviewer's earlier call)
findings.description: Throws InvalidArgumentException at request-time (not boot-time) on `session: symfony2`. Architecture reviewer argues this is the wrong choice vs auto-alias with E_USER_DEPRECATED for one minor. Simplicity reviewer (in plan brainstorm) argued the opposite — just cut hard, no shim. Real disagreement.
findings.recommendation: Judgment call. The plan (and commit 8abed63) follows simplicity. Keep as-is; document the hard cut in UPGRADE-5.0.md (already done). Flag for human reviewer to weigh in if they want auto-alias instead. Default: accept as-is.

findings.title: readonly / constructor-promotion inconsistency across injected-dep classes
findings.author: pattern-recognition-specialist
findings.description: Request::$mink is readonly but structurally-identical BrowserKit::$mink, RestContext::$request, JsonContext::$httpCallResultPool, HttpCallResult::$value are not. Rector promoted some but left others.
findings.recommendation: Either apply readonly uniformly across these classes, or remove it from Request.php so the whole library is consistent. Fix in PR2 as part of f2 cleanup (re-running Rector with the right set).

findings.title: Rector sweep uneven — TableContext has zero typed parameters
findings.author: pattern-recognition-specialist
findings.description: TableContext has 6 public methods with untyped params ($count, $table, $text, $colIndex, $rowIndex). Rector's typeDeclarations set should have added scalar hints (int, string) but didn't. Suggests either (a) Rector ran with a more conservative set than intended, or (b) the method signatures match an interface Rector dared not narrow.
findings.recommendation: Investigate alongside f2. Either widen rector.php's typeDeclarations coverage or add the hints manually. Fix in PR2.

findings.title: Missing atoum integration test for header round-trip under BrowserKit
findings.author: architecture-strategist (H2)
findings.description: The Goutte→BrowserKit cutover removed the `method_exists($client, 'setHeader')` branch. Under Symfony BrowserKit HttpBrowser, only whitelisted $_SERVER keys propagate to outbound symfony/http-client requests. A custom header like `X-Foo` may not actually reach the wire. No test asserts this.
findings.recommendation: Add tests/units/HttpCall/BrowserKit.php with a round-trip test: set a custom header via setHttpHeader, send a request to a mock endpoint, assert the header is received on the wire. Fix in PR2 or PR3 — reasonable either way.

findings.title: symfony2 exception message reveals internal version info
findings.author: security-sentinel
findings.description: InvalidArgumentException mentions `behatch/contexts 5.0` and `fabpot/goutte is abandoned`. For a test-only dev library this is actually helpful developer UX, not exploitable attack surface.
findings.recommendation: Accept as-is. The specificity helps downstream engineers fix their behat.yml quickly.

findings.title: No Dependabot config for github-actions
findings.author: security-sentinel
findings.description: .github/dependabot.yml absent. SHA-pinned actions will ossify without automated update PRs.
findings.recommendation: Defer to PR3 — the plan specifies a comprehensive Dependabot config (composer + github-actions, grouped security + minor updates). Mentioning here for completeness.

findings.title: No CodeQL / PHPStan yet (PR3-deferred)
findings.author: security-sentinel + architecture-strategist
findings.description: No static analysis wired up yet. Scrutinizer was deleted, nothing replaces it in PR2.
findings.recommendation: Deferred to PR3 — plan specifies PHPStan L8 + CodeQL for PHP with weekly + on-push schedule. Fix in PR3.

findings.title: UPGRADE-5.0.md verbose for internal-only consumption
findings.author: code-simplicity-reviewer
findings.description: 104 lines. Requirement table, behat.yml before/after, composer.json diff duplicate information in composer.json and CHANGELOG.md.
findings.recommendation: Optional simplification: trim to ~15 lines covering only the genuine API-break paragraphs (Goutte removal, atoum namespace rename, json-schema 6 $error['constraint'] shape change). Judgment call — the verbosity is helpful for human consumers who may not have the full context. Accept as-is.

findings.title: CHANGELOG.md keepachangelog format overkill for one entry
findings.author: code-simplicity-reviewer
findings.description: Added/Changed/Removed/Security sections for a single [Unreleased] bump. Could be one `## 5.0` heading + 8 bullets.
findings.recommendation: Judgment call. The format will pay off when we have multiple versions to document; premature for a single bump. Accept as-is.

findings.title: Request::__call uses call_user_func_array instead of direct dispatch
findings.author: performance-oracle
findings.description: `call_user_func_array([$this->getClient(), $name], $arguments)` is ~15-20% slower than `$this->getClient()->$name(...$arguments)` on PHP 8.4 microbench. __call is hot (once per behat step).
findings.recommendation: Change to `return $this->getClient()->$name(...$arguments);` if preserved. Micro-optimization, fix in PR2 if trivial.

findings.title: Package identity: fork still named behatch/contexts (not zignaly/behatch-contexts)
findings.author: architecture-strategist
findings.description: The Zignaly fork jumping from 3.x to 5.x with the same `behatch/contexts` name risks install-time collision if any Zignaly repo transitively requires upstream behatch/contexts. Strategic decision beyond PR2 scope.
findings.recommendation: Strategic — not a PR2 fix. Consider for a future org-level decision: rename to `zignaly-open/behatch-contexts` + keep `replace: behatch/contexts` mapping. Mark as out-of-scope.

findings.title: CI `php -l` step adds no value
findings.author: code-simplicity-reviewer
findings.description: atoum loads every src/ and tests/units/ file at startup; any parse error surfaces there. Rector's AST parse also catches syntax errors. `php -l` across all files adds N process spawns for zero additional coverage.
findings.recommendation: Drop the `Lint PHP` step. Fix in PR2 (trivial).

findings.title: .atoum.php xunit writer unused
findings.author: code-simplicity-reviewer
findings.description: The xunit report writer writes atoum.xunit.xml but the CI doesn't upload or consume it. Was useful when Travis forwarded xunit to Scrutinizer; neither is active now.
findings.recommendation: Drop the xunit writer/report block entirely (5 lines gone); keep `addDefaultReport()`. The namespace fix from this PR becomes moot. Fix in PR2.
